item.title: 10801 NW 97th St — Miami-Dade, FL (162000 SF)
item.description: 162K SF warehouse at Flagler Station in Miami-Dade, owned by AEW Capital Management, currently leased. Part of Miami Trading Zone industrial cluster.

item.title: 10Gfedsupply, LLC — ICE Mecklenburg, NC $704,997
item.description: Federal contract awarded to 10GFEDSUPPLY, LLC in NC ($704,996.57).

item.title: 11301 NW 97th Ave — Miami-Dade, FL (174400 SF)
item.description: 174K SF warehouse in Medley, Miami-Dade, owned by Hilco Redevelopment Partners, leased to Garland Food. Standard industrial property.

item.title: 12805 NW 42nd Ave — Miami-Dade, FL (235900 SF)
item.description: 236K SF Prologis distribution center in Opa-Locka, Miami-Dade, leased to Amazon. Active logistics property.

item.title: 13606 Furman Rd — Harris, TX (183000 SF)
item.description: 183K SF available warehouse in Houston, Harris County TX, divisible to 40K SF. Owned by Vault Distribution Center.

item.title: 1511 Zoo Pkwy — Duval, FL (250000 SF)
item.description: 250K SF Walmart distribution center in Jacksonville, Duval County FL, serving 18 Sam’s Clubs. Active retail logistics site.

item.title: 15810 Walgreens Dr — Palm Beach, FL (182800 SF)
item.description: 183K SF warehouse in Jupiter, Palm Beach FL, owned by Ambrose Property Group, leased to Niagara Bottling. Standard industrial property.

item.title: Howard Lutnick — Commerce Secretary, Newmark Officer/Director, ICE Warehouse Brokerage Conflict
item.description: Commerce Secretary Lutnick held $80M+ in Newmark stock for 8 months after confirmation while Newmark brokered ICE warehouse deals. Son Kyle now sits on Newmark board.

item.title: 142 Trump Administration Officials with Palantir Financial Ties
item.description: 142 Trump officials have Palantir financial ties, the largest conflict footprint of any detention contractor. Includes revolving-door hires at HHS, State, DOD, and both OMB procurement chiefs.

item.title: 33 Trump Administration Officials with Blue Owl Capital Investments
item.description: 33 Trump administration officials hold Blue Owl Capital investments per ProPublica disclosures, including Trump ($5M+) and SecNav Phelan, whose command administers WEXMAC-TITUS.

item.title: 54 Congressional Democrats Launch Investigation into ICE Warehouse Profiteering
item.description: 54 Congressional Democrats led by Raskin and Warren launched investigation into ICE warehouse profiteering on March 30, 2026. Documented 333-1000% markups and 33 admin members with Blue Owl investments.

item.title: Antoine McCord — DHS CIO, Former Anduril Employee with Active Equity
item.description: DHS CIO Antoine McCord is a former Anduril employee holding active Anduril equity. Paired with ex-Palantir OMB CIO Barbaccia, both Thiel-network alumni control federal tech procurement.

item.title: Blue Owl Capital — Private Equity Firm Profiting from ICE Warehouse Purchases
item.description: Blue Owl Capital ($307.5B AUM) sold distressed warehouses to DHS at multiples of market value. Stock crashed 68% amid $5.4B in redemption requests; DHS sales were a financial lifeline.

item.title: Brendan Rogers (Asst SecNav) — Loan Receivables from CBRE Logistics Partners AND JLL Partners Fund IX
item.description: Asst SecNav Rogers has loan receivables from CBRE and JLL (both brokered ICE warehouse deals) plus Blue Owl stock, while his department administers WEXMAC-TITUS procurement.

item.title: Carlyle Group — Oakwood GA Warehouse Seller, 22 Admin Officials Hold Investments
item.description: Carlyle Group sold the Oakwood, GA warehouse to DHS for $68.2M. 22 Trump officials hold Carlyle investments, including former Carlyle employee Todd Newnam now at Treasury.

item.title: Comprehensive Conflict-of-Interest Map: 11 ICE Warehouse Sellers × Trump Administration Officials
item.description: Cross-reference of all 11 ICE warehouse sellers against Trump official financial disclosures. GSA Admin Forst’s former employers Goldman and Deutsche Bank sold $274.7M in warehouses.

item.title: David Venturella
item.description: Spent 12 years at GEO Group ($6M+ pay), now runs ICE detention contracting with an ethics waiver. Since his return, GEO won $520M in new contracts and reported 700% profit increase.

item.title: DHS Inspector General Launches Investigation into Noem/Lewandowski Contract Handling
item.description: DHS IG launched investigation into Noem/Lewandowski contract handling on March 26, 2026. Lewandowski allegedly solicited success fees from GEO Group; contract corruption was a catalyst for Noem’s firing.

item.title: DHS Secretary Mullin Pauses ICE Warehouse Purchases, Reviews All Noem-Era Contracts
item.description: DHS Secretary Mullin paused 11 ICE warehouse conversions and imposed $25M contract approval threshold on March 24, 2026, reviewing all Noem-era contracts after $364M in documented overpayments.

item.title: Durant OK — Choctaw Nation Purchases Warehouse to Block ICE Detention Facility
item.description: Choctaw Nation purchased a Durant OK warehouse targeted by DHS, directly blocking ICE acquisition. Novel use of tribal sovereignty and economic power to remove property from federal reach.

item.title: Edward D'Alelio — Blue Owl Capital Director, Former Trump Entertainment Resorts Board
item.description: Blue Owl Capital director who previously served on the Trump Entertainment Resorts board, connecting the private equity firm profiting from ICE warehouses to the Trump business network.

item.title: Edward Forst — GSA Administrator, Former Goldman Sachs AND Deutsche Bank Employee
item.description: GSA Administrator and former Goldman Sachs partner whose agency’s procurement role was bypassed via WEXMAC-TITUS. All $1.074B in warehouse purchases closed after his Dec 2025 swearing-in.

item.title: FOIA and Open Records Requests Needed — Warehouse Profiteering Investigation
item.description: Prioritized list of FOIA and state open records requests targeting NDAs with local officials, missing appraisals, WEXMAC-TITUS scope modifications, and Blue Owl ethics determinations.

item.title: Full ICE Contract Pipeline — Conflict of Interest Map Across All Stages
item.description: Maps financial conflicts across all 7 stages of the ICE pipeline, from Palantir targeting (142 officials) to detention ops. Every stage has officials with financial ties to contractors.

item.title: GEO Group Financial Ties to Trump Administration Officials
item.description: GEO Group ties to Trump officials: Homan received GEO Care consulting fees as Border Czar, Venturella went from 12 years at GEO to running ICE detention contracting with ethics waiver.

item.title: Goldman Sachs — Double Exposure in ICE Warehouse Profiteering
item.description: Goldman Sachs has double ICE exposure: direct seller of Roxbury NJ ($129.3M, 137% markup) and lender on Williamsport MD ($352.7M refi). Combined Goldman-connected proceeds: $231.7M.

item.title: Goldman Sachs $352.7M Fundrise Refinancing — 12-Property Portfolio Analysis
item.description: Goldman Sachs and TPG refinanced Fundrise’s 12-property industrial portfolio for $352.7M in June 2025. Only Williamsport, MD was sold to DHS; remaining properties fit future ICE targets.

item.title: John Russell McGranahan — Former GSA General Counsel with Blue Owl Investments
item.description: Former GSA General Counsel (through Nov 2025) who held Blue Owl Capital investments while at the agency that normally brokers federal real estate. Blue Owl sold Tremont PA for $119M.

item.title: PNK Group — Russia-Founded Industrial Developer, Social Circle GA Seller, Sberbank Partner
item.description: Russia-founded industrial developer that sold Social Circle, GA warehouse to DHS for $128.6M after buying it for $29.4M. Founder Sharkov has Sberbank, Roscongress, and ICIJ offshore ties.

item.title: Project Salt Box — ICE Warehouse Overpayment Research and Tracking
item.description: Activist research org documenting ICE warehouse overpayments through property records and lien filings. Their data shows markups ranging from 137% to ~1000% across confirmed purchases.

item.title: Rockefeller Group (Mitsubishi Estate) — Surprise AZ Warehouse Seller
item.description: Mitsubishi Estate subsidiary that sold the Surprise, AZ warehouse to DHS for $70M vs ~$12M cost basis (483% markup). GardaWorld won the $313.4M conversion contract despite no detention experience.

item.title: Roxbury NJ Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: $129.3M warehouse (470K sq ft) in Roxbury NJ purchased at 137% markup from Goldman Sachs subsidiary. Governor, AG, and township jointly sued; bipartisan backlash in NJ.

item.title: Salt Lake City UT — ICE Mega Center (833,000 sq ft warehouse)
item.description: $145.4M purchase of 833K sq ft warehouse in Salt Lake City for 7,500-10,000 bed mega center. Sold by Deutsche Bank subsidiary at 50%+ markup; city imposed water restrictions to resist.

item.title: SEC Filing Analysis — Fundrise East Coast REIT and RREEF Core Plus Industrial Fund
item.description: SEC filing forensics on Fundrise (Williamsport MD) and RREEF/Deutsche Bank (SLC UT) warehouse ownership chains. Fundrise investors lost 33% while property was sold to DHS.

item.title: Social Circle GA Warehouse — ICE Mega Detention Center
item.description: $129M warehouse in Social Circle GA (valued at $29M, 333% markup) planned as 7,500-10,000 bed mega center. City locked water meter because 1M gal/day sewage demand exceeds town’s 660K capacity.

item.title: Socorro TX Warehouse — ICE Processing Center (~1000% Markup)
item.description: ICE warehouse in Socorro, TX (El Paso County) purchased for $123M despite $11M prior valuation, a ~1000% markup and the most extreme overpayment documented in the ICE warehouse program.

item.title: Surprise AZ Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: ICE warehouse in Surprise, AZ (Maricopa County), 550 beds, purchased for $70M vs $12M prior value (~500% markup). Contractor awarded the project has no detention background.

item.title: The Warehouse Profiteering Pattern — How Institutional Investors Extract Taxpayer Wealth Through ICE Purchases
item.description: Documents how ICE warehouse purchases function as wealth transfers: institutional investors buy distressed properties, DHS purchases at enormous markups via WEXMAC-TITUS, extinguishing bank debt with taxpayer funds.

item.title: Thomas Homan (Border Czar) — Complete Conflict Portfolio Across ICE Contractor Categories
item.description: Border Czar Tom Homan holds financial ties spanning every ICE contractor category: GEO Care fees, LexisNexis/RELX stock, CBRE stock, Prologis stock, and Blackstone stock.

item.title: Tremont PA Warehouse — ICE Mega Detention Center
item.description: Former Big Lots warehouse in Tremont, PA (Schuylkill County), 7,500-10,000 beds, bought from Blue Owl Capital for $119M (~2x market value). 33 Trump officials hold Blue Owl investments.

item.title: WEXMAC-TITUS — How ICE Bypasses Normal Contracting Using Military Procurement
item.description: WEXMAC-TITUS is a Navy contracting vehicle repurposed for ICE detention, expanded from $10B to $65B ceiling. Bypasses competitive bidding, appraisals, and transparency required under normal GSA procurement.

item.title: Williamsport MD — 1,500-Bed ICE Detention Center (Goldman Sachs / Fundrise)
item.description: Vacant warehouse in Williamsport, MD, 1,500 beds, owned by Fundrise and refinanced by Goldman Sachs months before DHS purchase. Judge extended restraining order against operations.

item.title: Ahtna Technical Services (Ahtna, Inc.)
item.description: Ahtna Technical Services is an Alaska Native Corp subsidiary using 8(a) sole-source contracting to operate ICE facilities including Port Isabel TX. ~$800M in historical contracts.

item.title: Alexandria Staging Facility — ICE's Deportation Flight Cornerstone
item.description: 400-bed GEO Group facility on a former Air Force Base tarmac in Alexandria LA, cycling 1,000+ people weekly. Cornerstone of ICE deportation flight operations and largest ICE Air hub.

item.title: Alligator Alcatraz — South Florida Detention Facility (Dade-Collier)
item.description: 3,000-5,000 bed state-operated facility built in 8 days in the Florida Everglades, opened July 2025. Documented abuse, maggot-infested food, and legal challenges; costs $450M/year to operate.

item.title: Apex Site Services
item.description: Apex Site Services is a North Texas construction firm that pivoted from COVID testing sites to proposing soft-sided ICE detention facilities, including in Walsenburg CO.

item.title: Babel Street — Location Data & Social Media Surveillance for ICE
item.description: Babel Street sells ICE location tracking (Locate X) and social media monitoring (Babel X) using ad-tech data harvested from phone apps. No warrant required.

item.title: Baker County Detention Facility — North Florida 'Deportation Depot
item.description: 1,300+ bed county jail in Sanderson FL (former state prison), opened Aug 2025 with GEO Group management. Part of Florida’s IGSA detention network; dubbed ‘Deportation Depot’ by protesters.

item.title: BI Incorporated
item.description: BI Incorporated (GEO Group subsidiary) runs ICE’s ankle monitor and SmartLINK surveillance program for 180,000+ people, plus a $121M skip-tracing contract to hunt non-compliant targets.

item.title: BI Incorporated — ISAP V IDIQ Contract (Ankle Monitors & Surveillance)
item.description: $1B sole-source contract for BI Inc (GEO subsidiary) to run ICE’s ISAP V ankle monitor and SmartLINK surveillance program. ICE targets 180,000 on GPS monitors by 2026.

item.title: BI Incorporated — Skip Tracing Contract ($121M)
item.description: $121M contract for BI Inc to hunt immigrants who missed check-ins using its own ISAP surveillance data. Completes GEO Group’s monitor-hunt-detain closed loop.

item.title: Bradford County FL — 3,000-Bed IGSA Facility on Contaminated Site
item.description: Community in Bradford County FL contested a 3,000-bed IGSA facility on a VOC-contaminated site pitched by Sabot Consulting. Commission voted 3-2 to advance; lease on agenda before environmental studies complete.

item.title: Bradford County FL — Sabot Consulting IGSA Proposal ($239M/year at full capacity)
item.description: Sabot Consulting proposed a 3,000-bed phased IGSA detention facility in Bradford County FL worth $239M/year at full capacity. County commission voted 3-2 to advance.

item.title: Bradford County ICE Detention Campus (Douglas Building)
item.description: Proposed 3,000-bed IGSA facility in Starke FL on a contaminated 30-acre site, phased from 1,000 to 3,000 beds at $221-269/bed/day. Pitched by Sabot Consulting as county-led economic development.

item.title: California City Immigration Processing Center (CoreCivic)
item.description: 2,560-bed CoreCivic facility in Kern County CA generating ~$100M/year under a two-year ICE contract. One of the largest private detention centers in the country, reactivated Aug 2025.

item.title: Camp Atterbury IN — Military ICE Detention
item.description: Indiana National Guard base ~40 miles south of Indianapolis designated for up to 1,000 ICE detainees. Named in Defense Secretary Hegseth’s July 2025 letter; still in planning phase.

item.title: Camp Blanding FL — 'Deportation Depot' ICE Detention Center
item.description: Florida National Guard base near Starke planned for 2,000 ICE detainees, branded ‘Deportation Depot’ by DeSantis. Paused until Alligator Alcatraz fills up; state-operated model.

item.title: Camp East Montana — $1.2B Army Contract for ICE Tent Detention Camp
item.description: $1.2B Army contract for a tent detention camp at Fort Bliss TX, awarded to a no-experience contractor at a Virginia residential address. Three deaths in first five months.

item.title: Camp East Montana — Fort Bliss ICE Tent Detention Camp
item.description: 3,000+ bed tent camp on Fort Bliss in El Paso TX, opened Aug 2025 under a $1.2B Army contract. Three deaths in custody, tuberculosis cases, and documented abuse; operator being replaced.

item.title: Capgemini Government Solutions
item.description: Capgemini won the largest ICE skip-tracing contract ($365M ceiling) to locate immigrants for deportation. The French multinational reportedly had internal doubts about reputational risk.

item.title: Central Louisiana ICE Processing Center, Jena LA
item.description: 1,160-bed GEO Group processing center in Jena LA, part of the Louisiana detention hub with Alexandria Staging Facility. Notable for holding detained college students including Mahmoud Khalil.

item.title: Clearview AI — $9.2M ICE facial recognition contract (Sept 2025)
item.description: $9.2M sole-source ICE contract with Clearview AI for facial recognition matching against a 50B+ image database. Justified for child exploitation and officer assault cases.

item.title: Clearview AI — Facial Recognition for ICE
item.description: Clearview AI provides ICE with facial recognition from a 50B+ image database via a $9.2M sole-source contract. Used with ’little apparent oversight’ per WBEZ.

item.title: Coast Guard Aircraft Diverted to ICE Detainee Transport (2025)
item.description: At least 324 Coast Guard flights diverted to ICE detainee transport in 2025, using C-27J Spartans and C-130s as feeders for Air Force deportation flights from hub airports.

item.title: Constellis Holdings (Blackwater successor)
item.description: Constellis (Blackwater/Academi successor) hunts immigrants for ICE under a $113M skip-tracing contract. Operatives conduct physical surveillance with no DHS credentials.

item.title: CoreCivic, Inc. (formerly CCA)
item.description: CoreCivic is the second-largest private prison company with $2.2B revenue in 2025, up 13%. Won ~$325M in new ICE contracts reactivating 6,353 beds across four facilities.

item.title: CSI Aviation
item.description: CSI Aviation manages ICE Air deportation flights, tripling to $1.1B in FY2025. Founded by Republican donor Allen Weh; subcontracts to GlobalX and World Atlantic.

item.title: Daniel A. Bible
item.description: Former ICE ERO deputy director who left days before the 2024 election to become GEO Group EVP. GEO invested $70M in detention expansion weeks after his hire, suggesting advance knowledge.

item.title: Daniel H. Ragsdale
item.description: Former ICE Deputy Director (2012-2017) who joined GEO Group as EVP two months after leaving government. Now SVP for Contract Administration and Compliance at GEO.

item.title: Delaney Hall — Newark NJ GEO Group ICE Facility
item.description: 1,000-bed GEO Group facility in Newark NJ reactivated in 2025 under a ~$1B contract. Part of GEO’s expansion from 20,000 to 26,000 ICE beds driving record $254M profit in 2025.

item.title: El Paso TX Eastwind Logistics Center — ICE Mega Detention Center
item.description: $123M warehouse purchase in Far East El Paso County planned as 8,500-bed mega center. Three warehouse buildings totaling ~888K sq ft; faces water infrastructure limits.

item.title: Endeavors (Family Endeavors)
item.description: Endeavors is a San Antonio nonprofit that received ~$3.2B in federal migrant services contracts under Biden. DOGE terminated a $215M contract for an empty Pecos facility.

item.title: G4S Secure Solutions — ICE Ground Transportation & Custody Contracts
item.description: G4S (Allied Universal) holds multiple ICE ground transportation task orders across six field offices. A $100M San Diego contract was solicited for FY2026.

item.title: G4S Secure Solutions (Allied Universal subsidiary)
item.description: G4S (Allied Universal subsidiary, formerly Wackenhut) provides armed ground transportation of ICE detainees across multiple field offices. $100M San Diego contract solicited.

item.title: GEO Group — WEXMAC-TITUS Pre-Qualification (No-Bid Access to $45B)
item.description: GEO Group was quietly pre-qualified for WEXMAC-TITUS, giving it no-bid access to $45B in OBBBA detention funding. Announced on a Friday night in Feb 2026.

item.title: Glades County Detention Center — Moore Haven FL
item.description: 500-bed county jail in Moore Haven FL with IGSA reinstated April 2025. Part of Florida’s county jail detention network; a 19-year-old detainee death drew media attention.

item.title: Glades County FL — Reinstated IGSA (up to 500 beds, April 2025)
item.description: ICE reinstated the Glades County FL IGSA on April 3, 2025 for up to 500 beds operated by the sheriff. Sabot Consulting featured it as a client success story.

item.title: GlobalX Airlines (Global Crossing Airlines)
item.description: GlobalX Airlines operates ~80% of ICE deportation flights, earning $246M in 2025. Flight attendants reported shackled passengers cannot be safely evacuated in emergencies.

item.title: Guantanamo Bay Migrant Operations Center (GMOC)
item.description: Trump ordered GMOC expansion from 130 to 30,000 capacity in Jan 2025, but fewer than 400 held at any time. Places immigration detainees outside normal federal court reach at estimated $40M/month.

item.title: Hagerstown MD Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: ICE purchased a Hagerstown MD warehouse for $100M+ in Jan 2026 as one of 16 regional processing centers. Part of the $38.3B Detention Reengineering Initiative targeting FY2026 completion.

item.title: Hamburg PA — ICE Processing Center (warehouse purchase)
item.description: Warehouse in Berks County PA purchased as one of 16 ICE regional processing centers with 1,000-1,500 bed capacity. Part of the Pennsylvania detention cluster with Upper Bern and Tremont.

item.title: Hanover VA — Warehouse Owners Terminated Deal with ICE
item.description: Warehouse owners in Hanover County VA terminated their deal with ICE after community pressure made the sale politically toxic. Part of a pattern of seller-side resistance.

item.title: ICE Air Operations — CSI Aviation Charter Flight Contract (FY2025-2026)
item.description: CSI Aviation’s ICE Air contract surged from $364M (FY2024) to $1.1B (FY2025). Domestic transfer flights jumped 132% as new remote detention facilities opened.

item.title: ICE Detention Medical Care Crisis (2025-2026)
item.description: 32 people died in ICE custody in 2025, the deadliest year in two decades. ICE stopped paying medical providers in Oct 2025, creating a ~$300M gap as detained population surged 82%.

item.title: ICE Skip Tracing Program — $1.2B across 13 contractors (Dec 2025)
item.description: $1.2B program across 13 private contractors to locate 1.5M immigrants for deportation using AI, databases, and physical surveillance. Operatives carry no DHS credentials.

item.title: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst NJ — Military ICE Detention
item.description: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst in NJ approved for up to 1,000 ICE detainees per Hegseth’s July 2025 letter. Governor Murphy opposed; no firm timeframe. NJ faces double expansion with Roxbury warehouse.

item.title: Kansas City MO — ICE Mega Center (BLOCKED by community pressure)
item.description: Planned 7,500-bed mega center in south Kansas City blocked when Platform Ventures withdrew after mass protests, student walkouts, and Port KC severing ties. Key seller-pressure resistance model.

item.title: Kansas City MO — Platform Ventures Warehouse Deal Blocked by Community Pressure
item.description: Community in Kansas City blocked a 7,500-bed mega center through student walkouts, mass protests, and a general strike. Platform Ventures withdrew Feb 12, 2026 after Port KC severed ties.

item.title: LexisNexis / RELX — Data Broker for ICE
item.description: LexisNexis sells ICE and CBP access to Accurint, a database of 10,000+ sources including utility records and license plates. Combined contracts worth up to $38M.

item.title: LexisNexis LEIDS — $22.1M ICE + $15.9M CBP database subscription
item.description: $38M combined ICE ($22.1M) and CBP ($15.9M) contracts for LexisNexis LEIDS database access including Accurint dossiers and license plate recognition.

item.title: Management & Training Corporation (MTC)
item.description: MTC is the third-largest private prison operator, pursuing a sole-source ICE contract for a shuttered Marana AZ prison. Pima County formally opposed it in Feb 2026.

item.title: Matthew T. Albence
item.description: Former Acting ICE Director (2019-2020) who joined GEO Group as SVP Client Relations. Known for calling family detention centers ‘more like summer camp.’ One of 6+ ex-ICE officials at GEO.

item.title: Merrimack NH — ICE Processing Center Paused After Community Opposition
item.description: Community in Merrimack NH blocked an ICE processing center. The fight produced leaked ICE planning documents revealing the 34-facility Detention Reengineering Initiative for the first time.

item.title: Merrimack NH Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: 43-acre warehouse in Merrimack NH planned as 500-1,500 bed ICE processing center at $300M estimated cost. Paused after community opposition in this swing-state town; ACLU obtained leaked ICE site plans.

item.title: Midwest Regional Reception Center — Leavenworth KS (CoreCivic)
item.description: 1,033-bed former federal prison in Leavenworth KS awarded to CoreCivic via no-bid contract. Delayed by zoning challenge; excluded from CoreCivic’s 2026 financial projections due to uncertainty.

item.title: Military Infrastructure in the Detention System — Three Distinct Uses
item.description: Military installations serve three distinct detention functions: active-base detention (avoiding civilian oversight), former-base staging facilities, and ICE Air deportation flight hubs.

item.title: MVM Inc. — ICE Detainee Transportation Contract ($1.1B cumulative)
item.description: $1.1B cumulative MVM Inc. contracts for ICE detainee and unaccompanied child transportation. $145M in new 2025 contracts; owner family are major Trump donors.

item.title: MVM, Inc.
item.description: MVM Inc. is a security contractor founded by ex-Secret Service agents with $1.1B in cumulative ICE transport contracts. Owners are major Trump donors.

item.title: NICE Systems — Call Recording & Video Surveillance
item.description: NICE Systems is an Israeli company providing phone recording, keyword search, and video surveillance to US prisons and jails. Specific ICE contract not yet confirmed.

item.title: North Lake Processing Center — Baldwin MI (GEO Group)
item.description: 1,800-bed GEO Group facility in Baldwin MI reactivated in 2025. Described as ’notorious’ for conditions; part of GEO’s expansion adding 6,000 beds to its ICE capacity.

item.title: Palantir ICM (Investigative Case Management) — $139.3M (2022-2026)
item.description: $139.3M Palantir contract for ICE’s Investigative Case Management system (2022-2026). Slated for sole-source renewal because no comparable system exists.

item.title: Palantir ImmigrationOS — $30M prototype + $29.9M maintenance (2025-2027)
item.description: $59.9M sole-source Palantir contracts to build ImmigrationOS, ICE’s deportation pipeline platform with AI targeting, confidence scoring, and self-deportation tracking.

item.title: Palantir Technologies — ICE Surveillance & Case Management Platform
item.description: Palantir builds ICE’s core surveillance infrastructure: ImmigrationOS deportation pipeline, ELITE targeting app, and ICM case management. Total ICE contracts ~$287M.

item.title: Romulus MI — State AG and City Sue to Block ICE Warehouse Near Schools
item.description: Michigan AG Nessel and City of Romulus sued to block an ICE warehouse purchased without notice near schools and in a floodplain. 700+ protesters rallied; Gov. Whitmer notably silent.

item.title: Romulus MI Warehouse — ICE Regional Processing Center
item.description: 500-bed ICE warehouse at 7525 Cogswell St, Romulus MI purchased Feb 2026 without public notice. Near schools and in a floodplain; Michigan AG and city filed lawsuit to block conversion.

item.title: Roxbury NJ — Governor, AG, and Township Sue to Block ICE Processing Center
item.description: NJ Governor, AG, and Roxbury Township jointly sued to block a 470K sq ft ICE warehouse. Project NINJA organized 1,500+ community members – broadest multi-level legal opposition of any facility fight.

item.title: Salt Lake City UT — Protests, Water Restrictions, and Mayor Opposition to 10,000-Bed Mega Center
item.description: Community in Salt Lake City contested a $145.4M mega center for 7,500-10,000 beds. City imposed water restrictions following Social Circle model; purchase already completed limits options.

item.title: San Antonio TX East Side Warehouse — ICE Processing Center
item.description: $66M+ warehouse (640K sq ft) on San Antonio’s East Side planned as 1,500-bed ICE processing center. Assessed value was $37M; part of the 24-warehouse Detention Reengineering Initiative.

item.title: ShadowDragon — Social Media Surveillance for ICE
item.description: ShadowDragon provides ICE with social media surveillance across 200+ websites including dating sites, forums, and social platforms. ICE plans a $20-50M monitoring office expansion.

item.title: Shakopee MN — Warehouse Owner Declined ICE Deal
item.description: Warehouse owner in Shakopee MN declined to sell to ICE after community opposition made the deal politically toxic. Follows the Kansas City seller-pressure model.

item.title: Social Circle GA — City Locked the Water Meter to Block 10,000-Bed Mega Center
item.description: Social Circle GA locked the water meter on a planned 10,000-bed mega center because its 1M gal/day sewage demand exceeded the city’s 660K gal/day capacity. Tactic replicated in Salt Lake City.

item.title: Surprise AZ — City Passed 5-Year Ban on Detention Facilities (Unenforceable Due to Federal Preemption)
item.description: Surprise AZ passed a 5-year ban on detention facilities after DHS bought a $70M+ warehouse without notice. Ban is unenforceable due to federal preemption – illustrates limits of post-purchase resistance.

item.title: The GEO Group, Inc.
item.description: GEO Group is the largest private detention company ($2.6B revenue, $254M profit in 2025) operating a closed loop: surveillance via BI, skip tracing, and 75,000 detention beds.

item.title: The ICE Contract Pipeline — From Targeting to Deportation
item.description: Framework mapping the 7-stage ICE enforcement pipeline from targeting to deportation, each served by different contractors. GEO Group operates a closed loop across surveillance, skip-tracing, and detention.

item.title: Thomson Reuters — CLEAR Data Broker for ICE
item.description: Thomson Reuters CLEAR gives ICE access to utility records, phone records, license plates, and real-time geolocation. $161M+ in cumulative DHS contracts since 2003.

item.title: Thomson Reuters CLEAR — $22.8M DHS/ICE database subscription (through 2026)
item.description: $22.8M five-year DHS contract for Thomson Reuters CLEAR database access. 2025 option confirms license plate reader data provision alongside utility and phone records.

item.title: Tracking Resources — Maps, Monitors, and Databases
item.description: Curated list of external resources for monitoring detention expansion: facility maps (Courier, Freedom for Immigrants), flight monitors (Human Rights First), investigative journalists, and FOIA tools.

item.title: Upper Bern Township PA Warehouse — ICE Processing Center
item.description: ICE processing center in Upper Bern Township, PA (Berks County), purchased for $87M in Feb 2026. Legal questions raised about local authority to block federal detention facilities.

item.title: Venntel / Gravy Analytics — Ad-Tech Location Data for ICE
item.description: Venntel sells ICE precise cell phone location data harvested from advertising bid-streams without warrants. FTC filed a complaint in Dec 2024 but ICE expanded purchases.

item.title: Vigilant Solutions (Motorola Solutions) — License Plate Reader Network for ICE
item.description: Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) operates the largest US license plate reader network. ICE has a $22.8M database contract plus purchased mobile ALPR hardware for field operations.

item.title: Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) — $22.8M ICE ALPR database access (through 2026)
item.description: $22.8M ICE contract for Vigilant Solutions (Motorola) license plate reader database access through 2026. ICE also purchased mobile ALPR hardware for ERO field operations.

item.title: World Atlantic Airways
item.description: World Atlantic Airways is a Miami-based overflow carrier for ICE deportation flights, subcontracting under CSI Aviation. Increased role after iAero Airways bankruptcy in 2023.
